coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of how large language models generate text token by token
Ability to call a hosted LLM through its API and read the completion
Elementary knowledge of writing zero-shot and few-shot prompts from examples
Ability to run a text-to-image diffusion model from an existing notebook
Basic understanding of temperature, top-p and max tokens sampling parameters
Ability to fill the variables of a prompt template under supervision
Elementary knowledge of reading generation logs to spot obvious failures
Basic understanding of what a token is and how it maps to billing
complementaryCompetencies: Basic knowledge of Python and Jupyter notebooks
Familiarity with REST APIs and JSON payloads
Elementary understanding of environment variables for API keys
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Can run existing generation scripts with detailed guidance
Needs 3-6 months to be productive

coreCompetencies: Ability to build a retrieval-augmented generation prototype over a document set
Practical knowledge of chunking documents and creating embeddings for retrieval
Ability to design few-shot prompts with clear instructions and output format
Understanding of prompt templating and the split between system and user messages
Ability to run text-to-image or image-to-image diffusion pipelines with custom parameters
Knowledge of measuring output quality with simple reference-based metrics
Ability to handle API errors, rate limits and retries in generation code
Understanding of context windows and how to fit prompts within token limits
complementaryCompetencies: Basic knowledge of vector databases such as FAISS or pgvector
Familiarity with Hugging Face libraries for loading models
Basic understanding of structured output and JSON schema validation
indicators: Can build simple generation features independently
Resolves medium complexity issues in prompts and retrieval
Actively participates in code and prompt reviews

coreCompetencies: Ability to build production RAG pipelines with reranking and source citation
Ability to fine-tune a model with LoRA on a task-specific dataset
Deep knowledge of prompt techniques such as chain-of-thought and structured output
Ability to design evaluation sets that measure groundedness and answer relevance
Understanding of guardrails that filter unsafe or off-topic generations
Ability to control inference cost by choosing model size and caching responses
Knowledge of ControlNet and conditioning techniques for diffusion image generation
Ability to instrument generation pipelines with tracing and token accounting
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of prompt injection risks and input sanitization
Ability to build human review workflows for generated content
Basic understanding of quantization for serving open-weight models
indicators: Completes complex generation features with minimal supervision
Identifies and resolves failures in retrieval and prompting
Actively contributes to technical design of generation systems

coreCompetencies: Ability to design end-to-end RAG architectures with hybrid search and query rewriting
Mastery of fine-tuning strategies including LoRA, QLoRA and full fine-tuning trade-offs
Ability to build automated evaluation suites scoring quality, toxicity and groundedness
Deep knowledge of serving open-weight LLMs with quantization and batching
Ability to design content safety frameworks covering input and output moderation
Mastery of inference cost optimization across model routing and caching
Ability to fine-tune diffusion models with techniques such as DreamBooth and LoRA
Knowledge of dataset curation and synthetic data generation for training
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of distillation to produce smaller task-specific models
Ability to design prompt management and versioning systems
Understanding of watermarking and provenance for generated media
indicators: Leads development of critical generation components
Effectively mentors junior engineers
Makes informed architectural decisions on generation design

coreCompetencies: Expertise in designing production generative AI platforms serving many use cases
Ability to architect scalable serving with GPU autoscaling and continuous batching
Mastery of evaluation-driven development with offline benchmarks and online A/B tests
Deep knowledge of red teaming generative systems for jailbreaks and unsafe output
Ability to design fine-tuning and alignment pipelines including preference tuning
Expertise in optimizing the quality, cost and latency trade-off at scale
Ability to build observability capturing prompts, retrievals and generation quality
Mastery of content safety governance including policy definition and escalation
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of reinforcement learning from human feedback and direct preference optimization
Ability to design domain-specific generation benchmarks from scratch
Understanding of licensing and copyright concerns for training data and outputs
indicators: Designs generation architectures for complete products
Defines team technical standards for generative AI development
Leads cross-team initiatives on generation infrastructure

coreCompetencies: Ability to architect enterprise generative AI platforms shared across many teams
Expertise in governance frameworks for safe deployment of generative models
Mastery of evaluation infrastructure and continuous quality and safety monitoring
Ability to lead build-versus-fine-tune-versus-buy decisions for foundation models
Deep knowledge of security threats specific to generative systems such as data leakage
Ability to lead adoption of generative features across existing products
Expertise in cost governance across large-scale inference and training budgets
Mastery of reliability engineering for non-deterministic generation services

Ability to design reusable prompt and evaluation catalogs for the organization
Understanding of on-premise and private deployment of generative models
indicators: Architects generation solutions for multiple teams

coreCompetencies: Technical leadership of organization-wide generative AI adoption

Expertise in strategies for safely scaling generative features to millions of users
Mastery of model-agnostic abstractions across proprietary and open-weight models
Ability to define multi-year roadmaps for generative AI capabilities
Deep knowledge of the economics and total cost of generative systems
Expertise in building and scaling generative AI engineering teams
Ability to set content safety and responsible generation standards across the org

Ability to shape internal policy on acceptable generative AI use

Mastery of technical due diligence for generative AI products and acquisitions
Ability to anticipate shifts in foundation models and adapt architecture strategy
